issue: Anti-pattern: waitForTimeout usage contradicts established best practices
reason: PR #2933 identified waitForTimeout as an anti-pattern that Issue #1493 eliminated. The code review comment stated: 'Should use event-driven waiting: await page.waitForURL(/\/demo/, { timeout: 5000 }) or await page.waitForLoadState(networkidle)'. PR #2949 uses page.waitForTimeout(500) in multiple places (lines with 'Wait for metrics grid to stabilize', 'Wait for error UI to stabilize', 'Wait for initial render but before data loads')
recommendation: Replace all waitForTimeout() calls with event-driven waiting patterns from WaitHelper.ts or Playwright's built-in waitForLoadState(), waitForResponse(), or element visibility checks

issue: Documentation location violates CLAUDE.md File Organization rules
reason: PR #2933 review feedback stated: 'Best practices documentation should be in docs/05-testing/ per CLAUDE.md structure.' However, there's already a comprehensive visual-regression.md file in docs/05-testing/. The new admin-dashboard-visual-regression.md creates documentation duplication and may confuse developers about which guide to follow
recommendation: Consider merging admin dashboard specific content into the existing visual-regression.md file as a section, or clearly differentiate the purpose if separate documentation is truly needed. The existing file already covers Admin Dashboard components comprehensively

issue: Potential code duplication with existing visual test patterns
reason: PR #2933 identified code duplication issues: 'This new waitForResponse() method duplicates functionality already in WaitHelper.waitForApiResponse()'. While this specific method may not be duplicated in PR #2949, the test file should leverage existing visual test patterns from e2e/visual/admin-dashboard.visual.spec.ts (established in PR #2906) rather than creating parallel implementations
recommendation: Review existing e2e/visual/admin-dashboard.visual.spec.ts from PR #2906 to ensure consistency in test structure, viewport handling, and screenshot configuration. Avoid reinventing patterns that already exist

issue: Package.json script naming inconsistency with established conventions
reason: PR #2949 adds test:e2e:visual:admin scripts, but existing visual tests use the e2e/visual/ directory structure. The new scripts reference e2e/admin-dashboard-visual.spec.ts which breaks the established file organization pattern where visual tests live in e2e/visual/. This creates confusion about where visual regression tests should be located
recommendation: Move admin-dashboard-visual.spec.ts to e2e/visual/ directory to match existing organization (admin-dashboard.visual.spec.ts already exists there). Update package.json scripts to use consistent naming: test:e2e:visual or test:e2e:visual:admin (if admin-specific scripts are needed)

issue: Documentation duplication: Two admin dashboard visual regression docs
reason: PR #2906 already established comprehensive visual regression documentation in docs/05-testing/visual-regression.md which includes a dedicated 'Admin Dashboard Components' section covering the same scope as the new document. Creating a second admin-dashboard-specific doc fragments knowledge and creates maintenance burden
recommendation: Either: 1) Merge unique content from admin-dashboard-visual-regression.md into the existing visual-regression.md as enhanced admin sections, OR 2) Clearly differentiate if admin-dashboard-visual-regression.md serves a genuinely different purpose (e.g., admin-specific workflows vs general visual testing). Current overlap includes: technology stack, workflow, baseline management, tolerance guidelines

reason: PR #2942 review identified missing dependencies: 'Missing react-confetti dependency added in da8ad81c6'. While PR #2949 doesn't add new component dependencies, the package.json changes should verify all Playwright visual testing dependencies are present (playwright, @playwright/test at correct versions)
recommendation: Verify Playwright and related dependencies are at compatible versions in package.json. Run pnpm install to ensure lockfile is updated if versions changed
